sentiment.rationale: Introduces the purpose of Proverbs and wisdom's urgent plea, warning against rejecting counsel and the destruction of fools.
Promises that seeking wisdom leads to understanding, protection, and deliverance from evil paths.
Encourages trust in the Lord, promising long life, peace, and blessings to those who embrace wisdom.
A father's instruction to pursue wisdom and avoid the path of the wicked, contrasting light and darkness.
A stern warning against adultery and the strange woman, urging fidelity and the consequences of lust.
Warnings against surety, sloth, and the seven abominations, with strong admonitions against adultery.
A vivid narrative warning of the seductress leading the simple to destruction like an ox to the slaughter.
Wisdom personified declares her eternal nature, her presence at creation, and the blessings she bestows.
Contrasts wisdom's feast with folly's invitation, establishing that the fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom.
Contrasting proverbs of Solomon comparing the righteous and the wicked, the diligent and the slothful.
Antithetical proverbs on honesty, generosity, and the outcomes of righteousness versus wickedness.
Contrasts the wise and foolish in speech, work, and conduct, emphasizing truthfulness and diligence.
Proverbs on discipline, the value of instruction, and the consequences of neglecting wisdom and correction.
Contrasting the wise woman and the foolish, the fear of the Lord, and the path that seems right but leads to death.
Teaches that a soft answer turns away wrath, the eyes of the Lord are everywhere, and a merry heart is a continual feast.
Affirms divine sovereignty over human plans, warns that pride goes before destruction, and commends humility.
Proverbs on friendship, the value of cheerfulness, the folly of strife, and restraint in speech.
Teaches on the power of the tongue, the name of the Lord as a strong tower, and the value of true friendship.
Proverbs on poverty, patience, parental discipline, and the fear of the Lord leading to life and satisfaction.
Warnings against wine and contention, commending honesty, diligence, and trust in the Lord's guidance.
The Lord directs the king's heart; proverbs on justice, the wicked, and that obedience is better than sacrifice.
A good name is rather to be chosen than riches; instructions to train children and care for the poor.
Warns against gluttony, drunkenness, and envy; encourages buying truth and honoring parents.
Wisdom builds a house; the righteous fall seven times and rise again; do not rejoice when your enemy falls.
Proverbs of Solomon copied by Hezekiah's men on self-control, dealing with enemies, and apt speech.
Vivid proverbs on fools, sluggards, talebearers, and the dangers of deceit and flattery.
Proverbs on friendship, honest rebuke, the value of diligence, and that iron sharpens iron.
Contrasts the bold righteous with the fleeing wicked; proverbs on integrity, greed, and confession of sin.
Where there is no vision the people perish; proverbs on discipline, justice, anger, and the fear of man.
The words of Agur: confessions of human limitation, numerical proverbs, and observations on nature and folly.
King Lemuel's mother warns against vice and praises the virtuous woman whose worth is far above rubies.

missingCharacters.name: Agur
missingCharacters.description: Son of Jakeh, author of Proverbs 30, known for his humble confessions of human limitation and numerical proverbs.

summaries.summary: Solomon introduces the purpose of Proverbs — to know wisdom and instruction — and personified wisdom cries in the streets, warning that rejecting her counsel leads to calamity.
Seeking wisdom with the earnestness of searching for treasure leads to understanding the fear of the Lord and deliverance from evil paths.
Trust in the Lord with all thine heart; wisdom brings long life, peace, and prosperity, and the Lord disciplines those he loves.
A father exhorts his son to get wisdom above all else, contrasting the path of the just that shines brighter with the darkness of the wicked.
Warns against the strange woman whose lips drip honey but whose end is bitter, urging faithfulness to one's own wife.
Warnings against surety for a stranger, slothfulness, sowing discord, and the seven things the Lord hates, followed by admonitions against adultery.
A vivid warning portraying the seductress leading a naive young man to destruction through flattery and enticement.
Wisdom personified declares her eternal origin before creation, her role alongside God, and that those who find her find life and favor.
Wisdom and folly each prepare a feast and invite the simple; the fear of the Lord is declared the beginning of wisdom.
The first collection of Solomon's individual proverbs, contrasting the righteous and the wicked, the wise and the foolish, the diligent and the lazy.
Antithetical proverbs teaching that dishonest scales are an abomination, the generous soul shall prosper, and righteousness delivers from death.
Proverbs contrasting truth and falsehood, diligence and slothfulness, with the observation that a righteous man regards the life of his beast.
Teaches that he who spares the rod hates his son, hope deferred makes the heart sick, and the wealth of the sinner is laid up for the just.
The wise woman builds her house; there is a way which seems right to a man but the end thereof are the ways of death.
A soft answer turns away wrath; the eyes of the Lord are in every place; a merry heart makes a cheerful countenance.
The Lord directs a man's steps; pride goes before destruction and a haughty spirit before a fall; the hoary head is a crown of glory.
A merry heart does good like a medicine; a friend loves at all times; even a fool who holds his peace is counted wise.
The name of the Lord is a strong tower; a man who has friends must show himself friendly; there is a friend that sticks closer than a brother.
The fear of the Lord leads to life and contentment; he that has pity on the poor lends to the Lord; chasten thy son while there is hope.
Wine is a mocker; the spirit of man is the candle of the Lord; divers weights and measures are alike an abomination to the Lord.
The king's heart is in the hand of the Lord; to do justice and judgment is more acceptable than sacrifice; the horse is prepared for battle but safety is of the Lord.
A good name is rather to be chosen than great riches; train up a child in the way he should go; the beginning of the words of the wise.
Warnings against envy, gluttony, and drunkenness; buy the truth and sell it not; apply thine heart unto instruction.
Through wisdom a house is built; the righteous falls seven times and rises again; do not rejoice when thine enemy falls.
Proverbs of Solomon copied by Hezekiah's men: a word fitly spoken is like apples of gold; if thine enemy hunger, feed him.
Vivid comparisons on fools, sluggards, and talebearers: as a dog returns to his vomit, so a fool returns to his folly.
Boast not of tomorrow; iron sharpens iron; faithful are the wounds of a friend; be diligent to know the state of thy flocks.
The wicked flee when no man pursues but the righteous are bold as a lion; he that confesses and forsakes his sins shall have mercy.
Where there is no vision the people perish; the fear of man brings a snare; a child left to himself brings shame.
The words of Agur son of Jakeh: confessions of human limitation, a prayer for neither poverty nor riches, and numerical proverbs on things wonderful and small.
King Lemuel's mother counsels against vice and strong drink, followed by the acrostic poem praising the virtuous woman whose price is far above rubies.

crossReferences.note: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge/wisdom — a shared foundational principle.
Trust in the Lord with all thine heart parallels blessed is the man that trusteth in the Lord.
Quoted in Hebrews regarding the Lord's chastening of those he loves.
God resists the proud but gives grace to the humble — quoted directly by James.
Wisdom's presence at creation parallels the Word being with God in the beginning.
Finding wisdom finds life, paralleling Christ as the way, the truth, and the life.
The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life, echoing the tree of life in Revelation.
A soft answer turns away wrath parallels being slow to anger in James.
Pride goes before destruction parallels Edom's pride leading to downfall.
Training a child in the way he should go parallels the commandment to teach children the gospel.
If thine enemy hunger feed him — quoted directly by Paul.
Where there is no vision the people perish parallels the famine of hearing the word of the Lord.
The virtuous woman description parallels Boaz calling Ruth a virtuous woman.

verses.reason: 'The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge' — the foundational motto of the entire book.
'For the LORD giveth wisdom: out of his mouth cometh knowledge and understanding.'
'Trust in the LORD with all thine heart' — one of the most memorized verses in all scripture.
'In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths.'
'The path of the just is as the shining light, that shineth more and more unto the perfect day.'
'Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life.'
Warning that the strange woman's lips drip as honeycomb but her end is bitter as wormwood.
'These six things doth the LORD hate: yea, seven are an abomination' — the famous list of seven sins.
Wisdom declares 'The LORD possessed me in the beginning of his way, before his works of old' — key to premortal existence discussions.
'Then I was by him, as one brought up with him: and I was daily his delight.'
'The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom' — restates the book's central theme.
'Hatred stirreth up strifes: but love covereth all sins.'
'The blessing of the LORD, it maketh rich, and he addeth no sorrow with it.'
'There is that scattereth, and yet increaseth' — the paradox of generous giving.
'The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life; and he that winneth souls is wise.'
'A righteous man regardeth the life of his beast' — early teaching on kindness to animals.
'Hope deferred maketh the heart sick: but when the desire cometh, it is a tree of life.'
'He that spareth his rod hateth his son: but he that loveth him chasteneth him betimes.'
'There is a way which seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death.'
'Righteousness exalteth a nation: but sin is a reproach to any people.'
'A soft answer turneth away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger.'
'The eyes of the LORD are in every place, beholding the evil and the good.'
'Commit thy works unto the LORD, and thy thoughts shall be established.'
'Pride goeth before destruction, and an haughty spirit before a fall.'
'A friend loveth at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.'
'A merry heart doeth good like a medicine: but a broken spirit drieth the bones.'
'The name of the LORD is a strong tower: the righteous runneth into it, and is safe.'
'There is a friend that sticketh closer than a brother.'
'He that hath pity upon the poor lendeth unto the LORD; and that which he hath given will he pay him again.'
'There are many devices in a man's heart; nevertheless the counsel of the LORD, that shall stand.'
'Wine is a mocker, strong drink is raging: and whosoever is deceived thereby is not wise.'
'The spirit of man is the candle of the LORD, searching all the inward parts of the belly.'
'The king's heart is in the hand of the LORD, as the rivers of water: he turneth it whithersoever he will.'
'To do justice and judgment is more acceptable to the LORD than sacrifice.'
'A good name is rather to be chosen than great riches.'
'Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it.'
'For as he thinketh in his heart, so is he.'
'Buy the truth, and sell it not; also wisdom, and instruction, and understanding.'
'For a just man falleth seven times, and riseth up again: but the wicked shall fall into mischief.'
'A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in pictures of silver.'
'If thine enemy be hungry, give him bread to eat' — quoted by Paul in Romans 12:20.
'As a dog returneth to his vomit, so a fool returneth to his folly.'
'Faithful are the wounds of a friend; but the kisses of an enemy are deceitful.'
'Iron sharpeneth iron; so a man sharpeneth the countenance of his friend.'
'The wicked flee when no man pursueth: but the righteous are bold as a lion.'
'He that covereth his sins shall not prosper: but whoso confesseth and forsaketh them shall have mercy.'
'The rod and reproof give wisdom: but a child left to himself bringeth his mother to shame.'
'Where there is no vision, the people perish: but he that keepeth the law, happy is he.'
'Every word of God is pure: he is a shield unto them that put their trust in him.'
'Give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with food convenient for me.'
'Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above rubies.'
'Favour is deceitful, and beauty is vain: but a woman that feareth the LORD, she shall be praised.'
